data.title: Finsbury Growth & Income Trust Buys Back Shares, Updates Voting Rights
data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Finsbury Gwth & Inc Shs ( (GB:FGT) ).

Finsbury Growth & Income Trust PLC has repurchased 108,982 of its ordinary shares at 745.93 pence per share, with the shares to be held in treasury. This buyback reduces the number of shares in free circulation and leaves the trust with 108,619,018 voting shares in issue, a move that can support the share price and marginally enhance net asset value per share for remaining investors.

Following the latest transaction, the Company now holds 116,372,285 ordinary shares in treasury, increasing its capacity to manage capital structure actively. The updated voting-rights figure will serve as the reference denominator for shareholders assessing their holdings under FCA disclosure and transparency rules, clarifying ownership thresholds and regulatory reporting obligations.

Spark’s Take on FGT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FGT is a Neutral.

The score is driven by solid underlying financial strength (strong balance sheet and positive cash generation) but is meaningfully weighed down by unstable earnings that turned into a FY2025 loss and very weak technicals (below key moving averages with bearish momentum and oversold readings). Valuation is mixed due to the negative P/E, partially offset by a modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on FGT stock, click here.

More about Finsbury Gwth & Inc Shs

Finsbury Growth & Income Trust PLC is a UK-listed investment trust focused on delivering capital growth and income to shareholders through a portfolio of equities. It targets long-term investors seeking exposure to a diversified basket of companies, aiming to combine steady income distributions with potential for capital appreciation.

data.title: Cannabis Suisse Delays Quarterly SEC Filing
data.description:
Cannabis Suisse ( (CSUI) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cannabis Suisse Corp. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors that it will delay its Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the quarter ended February 28, 2026. The company’s required quarterly filing under the Securities Exchange Act will therefore not arrive on its original due date.

The company attributes the delay to its outside auditors, who are still completing their review of the financial statements. This indicates the holdup is driven by external audit work rather than disclosed internal control failures or management disputes.

Cannabis Suisse Corp. states that the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) will be filed no later than five calendar days after the prescribed due date. Investors should therefore expect a relatively short postponement, assuming the audit review concludes as planned.

Management does not expect any significant change in results of operations compared with the same quarter of the prior fiscal year. As with all forward-looking statements, this expectation may differ from the final numbers once the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) is filed and fully reviewed by regulators and investors.

The company affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling continued efforts to stay in compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed on behalf of Cannabis Suisse Corp. by CEO Scott McAlister, underscoring executive responsibility for the delayed filing and the plan to complete it promptly.

More about Cannabis Suisse

Average Trading Volume: 14,530

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $424.1K

For a thorough assessment of CSUI stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Baillie Gifford European Growth Trust PLC GBP ( (GB:BGEU) ) has issued an update.

Baillie Gifford European Growth Trust plc has repurchased 140,000 ordinary shares at 99.60 pence each, with the acquired stock to be held in treasury rather than cancelled. This move adjusts the balance between shares in issue and treasury holdings, which can influence supply-demand dynamics for the trust’s shares and forms part of its broader capital management strategy.

Following the transaction, the trust now holds 107,538,323 shares in treasury, leaving 294,905,367 shares in issue excluding treasury stock. This updated figure serves as the new denominator for shareholders when calculating notifiable stakes under the FCA’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, affecting how investors report changes in their ownership positions.

The latest update is out from Genuit Group ( (GB:GEN) ).

Genuit Group has granted nil-cost share options under its Long Term Incentive Plan to executive directors and several senior managers for the financial year starting 1 January 2026. Awards to chief executive Joe Vorih and chief financial officer Tim Pullen cover up to 430,744 and 299,248 shares respectively, with additional allocations to five other PDMRs across people, water, climate, strategy and legal functions.

These options, linked to performance measured from 2026 to 2028 and subject to a five-year holding period from grant, are designed to align management incentives with long-term shareholder value. The grants, disclosed in line with UK Market Abuse Regulation, underscore the company’s continued reliance on equity-based pay to retain key leaders and embed a long-term performance culture at the top of the organisation.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:GEN) stock is a Buy with a £394.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Genuit Group stock, see the GB:GEN Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GEN is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid but not standout financial performance (better margins and earnings, but uneven revenue, rising leverage vs prior years, and weaker cash conversion). This is offset by weak technical momentum with the stock trading below key moving averages and bearish MACD/oscillators. Valuation and a ~3.24% dividend yield provide moderate support.

To see Spark’s full report on GEN stock, click here.

data.title: Lithium Ionic Faces Director Ouster Push Amid OSC-Linked Governance Scrutiny
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Lithium Ionic Corp ( (TSE:LTH) ).

Lithium Ionic Corp. has acknowledged receiving a requisition letter from Waratah Capital Advisors requesting a special shareholder meeting to remove and replace three directors, David Gower, Lawrence Guy and Hélio Diniz. A special committee of independent directors has advised that the three directors’ resignations would be in the company’s best interest, and the board is reviewing the requisition while indicating shareholders need not take action yet.

The requisition and board review stem from an enforcement application initiated by the Ontario Securities Commission against certain directors of Lithium Ionic and another issuer, though the company itself is not a respondent and no orders have been sought against it. The independent special committee has been tasked with overseeing the company’s communications and disclosure related to the OSC matter, signaling heightened governance scrutiny and potential leadership changes as the company advances its Brazilian lithium project.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:LTH) stock is a Buy with a C$3.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lithium Ionic Corp stock, see the TSE:LTH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LTH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LTH is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (no revenue and ongoing cash burn despite improving losses). Offsetting this are improving technical momentum and positive corporate developments (feasibility report and fresh financing), while valuation remains constrained by negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on LTH stock, click here.

More about Lithium Ionic Corp

Lithium Ionic Corp. is a Canadian lithium development company focused on its 100%-owned Bandeira Lithium Project in Minas Gerais, Brazil, an emerging hard-rock lithium district known as “Lithium Valley.” The company aims to become a near-term producer of high-quality spodumene concentrate for global battery supply chains, emphasizing engineering de-risking, permitting, commercial planning and construction readiness.

Average Trading Volume: 537,639

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: C$151.9M

For an in-depth examination of LTH stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: JY Grandmark Sells Entire Stake in Subsidiary in Minor Portfolio Adjustment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from JY Grandmark Holdings Limited ( (HK:2231) ) is now available.

JY Grandmark Holdings Limited has agreed to sell 100% of the equity interest in an indirectly held subsidiary, referred to as the Target Company, to an independent purchaser under an equity transfer agreement signed on 14 April 2026. Upon completion, the Target Company will cease to be a subsidiary and its financial results, assets and liabilities will no longer be consolidated into the group’s financial statements.

The disposal, priced at RMB650,000 and settled in cash on the signing date, qualifies as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, requiring reporting and announcement but not shareholder approval. All debts and liabilities of the Target Group will remain with that group after completion, limiting ongoing obligations for JY Grandmark and signalling a small-scale portfolio adjustment rather than a transformational restructuring.

More about JY Grandmark Holdings Limited

JY Grandmark Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 2231. The group operates through various subsidiaries in Mainland China, with a corporate structure that allows it to hold and dispose of equity interests in its project companies as part of its capital and portfolio management strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 600,901

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$62.55M

An update from Oxford Nanopore Technologies PLC ( (GB:ONT) ) is now available.

Oxford Nanopore Technologies has reported routine employee share dealings under its Share Incentive Plan involving its chief executive officer, Francis Van Parys, and chief financial officer, Nick Keher. Both directors purchased 136 partnership shares each at £1.10 per share via the plan’s trustee, Computershare Trustees Limited, using their gross salary contributions.

In line with the plan’s terms, the company issued a further 136 matching shares for each director, which are held on their behalf by the trustee at no additional cost. The transactions, conducted on 14 April 2026 on the London Stock Exchange, marginally increase the executives’ equity stakes and reflect ongoing alignment of senior management incentives with shareholder interests under the UK Market Abuse Regulation disclosure regime.
